text:
There is reportedly a shortage of bottled water available to staff and athletes inside theOlympicVillage as another issue in Paris comes to the surface.

The athletes’ base has split opinion among sports stars in years gone by, with tennis aceCoco Gauffthe latest star to speak out against the facility after claiming that 10 women were forced to share two bathrooms.

Reports of a shortage in eggs and meat emerged earlier this week, two staples of an athletes’ diet – in most cases – given the need for protein. However, it is not just food which is said to be running low at the sporting hub.

According to theDaily Mail, there is now a shortage of bottled water in the Village and staff have been forced to ‘ration’ the supplies and what they can give out to ensure that they have enough to get through the next few days of immense heat.

The temperatures in the French capital have been blistering throughout the competition so far, pushing athletes to their very limits when competing. However, questions are said to have been raised about the water supplies internally.

Insiders are claimed to have blamed ‘a lack of information’ being provided to those from nations where using refillable bottles is not commonplace.

Don't miss:Transgender boxer wants Imane Khelif banned from Olympics as gender row explodes



Express Sport has approached the International Olympic Committee for comment.

The food hall has also been a major talking point among athletes when the Olympic Village has been discussed, with the size of the canteen leading to significant queues and an overwhelming sense of busyness.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy



Diving star Tom Daley is far from unfamiliar with how the Villages operate, though even he has raised his concerns about the facility. Speaking to the GQ Magazine, he said: “The village itself is actually quite great. The only thing is the food hall gets busy, but hopefully, they’ll figure that out because as the games go on it's just going to get busier and busier.

"You know the crazy thing about the Olympic Village is you bump into people all the time. Like the other day, we were talking towards the bus… [Rafael] Nadal was really late for the bus and he just ran right into the middle of the bus driveway. You know, he needed to make that bus, I don’t know if he had a match or what.”

title: Fresh Olympic Village drama as staff forced to ration water for athletes in searing heat | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Charlie Parker-Turner
description: The Olympic Village is reportedly continuing to cause issues for athletes in Paris.
